4 tokens registered: GoodDollarToken, UBIFeeSplitter (suspicious), token@0x9fE4..., token@0xCf7E...

getPrice() reverts for all 4 registered tokens. PriceData.timestamp=0 for all tokens, meaning prices were never actually pushed by a keeper after token registration. getPriceUnsafe() returns non-zero prices suggesting tokenConfigs have prices in wrong struct layout OR data was written directly to storage. getPrice() enforces staleness: age = now - 0 = very large, exceeds maxAge=300 → revert StalePrice. Oracle is completely broken for live queries.

All timestamps=0 — keepers never called updatePrice(). The prices shown via getPriceUnsafe are likely leftover from storage layout overlap. TWAP also returns non-zero values despite timestamp=0, which means getTWAP falls back to pd.price (correct fallback).
TWAP fallback path (twapWindowStart==0) returns spot price correctly per code logic. Technically passes but misleading since underlying price timestamp=0.
Tester wallet 0x90F79... is NOT authorized as keeper. Cannot push prices without deployer access.

GoodLendPool.getAddressesProvider() does not exist in the deployed ABI. GoodLendPool actual functions are: admin, borrow, flashLoan, getBorrowIndex, getLiquidityIndex, getReserveData, getReservesCount, getUserAccountData, initReserve, interestRateModel, liquidate, mintToTreasury, oracle, repay, reservesList, setAdmin, setBorrowingEnabled, setOracle, setReserveActive. No AddressesProvider pointer found. GoodLendAddressesProvider contract NOT found in any broadcast — appears undeployed. Test plan called for it based on incorrect assumption about Aave-style pool interface.
Oracle wired to 0x9a9f... (not the perp PriceOracle 0x0165...). Separate oracle for lending.
Admin is deployer. We cannot change oracle price.
VaultManager oracle is MockPriceOracle at known address.

MockPriceOracle.getPrice('WETH18') returns 0. Only 'USDC' ilk has price set (1e18). WETH18 and AAPL prices are both 0 in the oracle. VaultManager.healthFactor(WETH18, wallet) returns uint256.max (no debt open), which is correct for an empty vault. But any vault that tried to open with WETH18 collateral would use price=0, likely causing divide-by-zero or allowing infinite minting. Critical oracle setup failure.

GoodDollarBridgeL2 is NOT deployed on devnet. No broadcast file for it (only GoodDollarBridgeL1.sol source exists, no L2 deployment script in broadcasts). Cannot test l1Bridge(), messenger(), paused(), pendingETHWithdrawalTotal(). Bridge is undeployed. Action needed: create DeployGoodDollarBridgeL2.s.sol.

MultiChainBridge is NOT deployed. Only source file exists at src/bridge/MultiChainBridge.sol. DeployLiFiBridgeAggregator.s.sol broadcast exists but did not deploy MultiChainBridge. Bridge layer has zero on-chain presence beyond FastWithdrawalLP.

FastWithdrawalLP.ubiPool is set to the deployer address (0xf39f...) instead of the UBI pool. This means all UBI routing fees from fast withdrawals would be sent to the deployer EOA, not the UBI distribution contract. Also totalLiquidity() reverts (likely no ERC20 token configured, only ETH path works). totalETHLiquidity=0 means no liquidity provided yet.

CONFIRMED STILL BROKEN. eth_getStorageAt slot 0x4 = 0x0. ubiRecipient is still address(0). Bug GOO-196 marked done but NOT deployed. Any call to splitFee() that routes to ubiRecipient will send tokens to address(0), permanently burning them.
RESOLVED: poolManager is no longer 0x1. GOO-205 has been fixed by RedeployUBIFeeHook.s.sol. poolManager now points to 0xac9f... (real PoolManager). Bug GOO-205 can be marked fixed.
ubiPool is set to a non-zero address. Pool is configured.

UBIFeeHook.totalUBIFees() and feeSplitter() both revert with no data. These functions may not exist in the deployed version or the function selectors do not match. getHookPermissions() returned all zeros (no hook flags set), which means afterSwap callback is disabled. Hook effectively does nothing — swap fees won't be routed to UBI. The hook permissions bitmap should have afterSwap enabled.

CONFIRMED STILL BROKEN. feeSplitter points to 0xcf7ed3... which is NOT UBIFeeSplitter (0xe7f1...) AND NOT UBIFeeHook (0x85495...). It points to a fourth unknown contract. GOO-245 still unfixed. Revenue tracking is wired to wrong contract.

UBIRevenueTracker functions owner(), totalFees(), currentEpoch() all revert with no data. Contract is either bricked or ABI mismatch. Combined with wrong feeSplitter, UBIRevenueTracker appears non-functional.

GOO-224 PARTIAL FIX: PerpEngine.oracle() now correctly points to PriceOracle. However markets 2-5 wiring (oracle-per-market) may still be incorrect per original bug. On-chain oracle address matches expected — marking as fixed for this check.

MarketFactory.conditionalTokens() reverts — either function doesn't exist at this selector or contract is bricked. ConditionalTokens broadcast found at 0xdfde6b33... but MarketFactory has no working view functions (oracle(), owner(), conditionalTokens() all revert). Cannot confirm orphaned address. GOO-214 unresolved — contract state unreadable.

GoodPool[0] is active with ~3M tokenA and ~1000 tokenB in reserves. Ratio suggests 1 tokenB ≈ 3000 tokenA (like USDC/token pair). LP has grown slightly from iter5 (reserveB was 1000e18, now 999.67...).
GoodPool[1]: 1M tokenA (18 dec) vs 1M tokenB (6 dec). LP has 1e18 tokens. Minimal activity.
GoodPool[2]: 3M tokenA (6 dec) vs 1000 tokenB (18 dec). Active with liquidity.
AMM pricing works. 100e18 tokenA → ~0.033 tokenB. Consistent with 3000:1 ratio. AMM is functional.

40% error rate (20/50 calls failed). Failed calls were against contracts with missing function selectors (expected reverts in test design). Latency is excellent: p50=8.4ms, p99=70.8ms. If filtering to non-revert calls only: error rate would be lower. However the high revert rate indicates many contracts have broken ABIs or missing functions. True RPC health: excellent. Contract health: poor.
